programmes.show_description: Emergency nurses fight to save a man whose gone into anaphylactic shock. Transplant nurse Rodrigo leads a mission across Australia to retrieve a donor heart.
Hit the roads with the brave paramedics of the West Midlands Ambulance Service. Hannah and Mike are called to a young woman who has had a fit in the street.
Reality series following West Midlands ambulance workers. Michelle and Simon rush to a young man who has collapsed at work. Gaz and Debbie help a teenager with chest pain.
Michael McIntyre hosts as the contestants, guided by celebrity experts including Rachel Riley, Joel Dommett and Daisy Lowe, attempt to answer their way to a hefty prize.
Shopping from Home.
Fools Rush In: Arun is incensed when a gang on the Bronte estate sets upon a young couple - but he is taken in by one of the local youngsters, who draws him into a humiliating trap.
Loved and Lost: Leon freezes when a teenage girl is found beaten, bloodied and unconscious in an alleyway, and he is forced to confront his own demons as he investigates the case.
In Deep: A barrister's daughter with a cocaine habit is pushed out of a car by her supplier when she doesn't settle her debts. Terry goes undercover at the suspected dealer's cab company.
Proof of Life: Part 1: Working undercover as a cab driver, Terry is caught up in an international kidnap plot which forces Sun Hill to work closely with their continental colleagues.
Proof of Life: Part 2: Jack and his team follow the gang to Germany and help arrange a ransom pick-up at Leipzig railway station, but the case takes an alarming and violent turn.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
A high speed crash leaves a woman seriously injured and trapped in her crumpled car. Also, a man falls 20 ft through the roof of a Victorian factory.
Yorkshire's HART paramedics race to help a man who has been stabbed in the back with a screwdriver, and there's a fire in a hotel resulting in a desperate search for the people inside.
A man is left with a metal spike impaled in his face after a traumatic incident on a construction site, sparking a huge rescue operation. Plus, medics help a man who was hit by a car.
A woman plunges 10ft through the floorboards of her Sheffield home in a freak accident, and the HART team launch a major rescue operation to save her.
In Portsmouth's Queen Alexandra Hospital, newly-minted Matron Steph helps her team care for patients, including 79-year-old Gordon who suffered a sudden loss of consciousness at home.
Matron Hazera responds to an emergency and battles to find enough staff, while Renee treats a patient with a flare-up of ulcerative colitis and Sarah cares for a terminally ill patient.
Another round of fantastic comedy characters. In this show, Elaine Figgis seeks true love via a website, and the equally versatile Paul Whitehouse turns up as Sam's dad.
More from the award-winning comedy sketch series performed by the talented Ms Tate. Characters this time include Derek Faye, Lauren and a rather annoying waitress.
Catherine's back with another batch of quality sketches. Peter Kay does a turn as he visits Nan and together they hilariously render Bridge Over Troubled Water.
Father-of-three Chris undergoes high risk brain surgery and mental health nurse Kate is called into a police station to treat a confused woman at risk of self-harm.
Louis Theroux heads to America to investigate one of the most controversial and captivating icons of recent times: gun-toting, self-described 'gay hillbilly' and 'Tiger King' Joe Exotic.
Three contestants try to answer their way to a pile of life-changing cash, with a little help from some famous faces including Myleene Klass, Prue Leith and Stephen Mangan.
Shopping from Home.
The Mouthtrap: Murder most foul in the quirky ratings topper. One of Ben's patients, while under anaesthetic, admits to killing his wife. And so naturally, Susan turns sleuth.
While You Weren't Sleeping: The popular domestic sitcom starring Robert Lindsay and Zoe Wanamaker. There are problems in the bedroom which threaten to drive Ben and Susan apart.
Dentist to the Stars: Robert Lindsay and Zoe Wanamaker star in the family sitcom. One of Britain's best-loved heart throbs visits Ben's surgery. Peter Capaldi guest stars.
A Wife Less Ordinary: Comedy from the bonkers Harpers. Susan, bored with her lot, opts to run her life as a single girl again. Janey takes the opportunity to try running the home.
The Book of Love: Domestic sitcom with a great cast. Susan attends a neighbour's book club. Michael runs a booking agency exclusively for Roger, while Ben books a seat on the sofa.
Going Dental: Susan forsakes Ben's dental conference to write her novel. Ben seeks a new partnership in Harley Street but ends up with more partners than he had bargained for.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
Ben Shephard hosts the quiz show in which four players take on an extraordinary machine.
Marlee, Jethro & Muffin: Tea-drinking Bedlington Terrier Muffin has a fractured leg due to her limb deformity, and Labrador Jethro's arthritic elbows are stopping him from walking properly.
Lexie, Charlie & Locky: Maine Coon Lexie needs a bilateral total hip replacement, while bouncy Border Terrier Charlie is struggling to walk and dwarf rabbit Locky has broken his leg.
Oz, Ollie & Bayleigh: Labrador puppy Ollie is rushed in after being hit by a car, while Bullmastiff Bayleigh has a painful lump near her ankle and adventurous cat Oz's leg needs saving.
Merida, Tito & Monti: Rough Collie puppy Merida has deformed front legs, fidgety Cockapoo Monti has fractured his knee and Weimaraner Tito is brought in after being hit by a van.
Rogan, Brodie & Vinnie: Noel treats Boston Terrier Rogan's debilitating dysplasia and creates an implant for Hungarian Vizsla Brodie, while a cat has leg injuries after being hit by a car.
The paramedics treat a young mum with severe stomach pains, take a breathless pensioner to hospital and attend an elderly lady with dementia whose nose will not stop bleeding.
Dudley crews attend a man with heart failure and sepsis, as well as a transgender patient who's in a lot of pain from an old injury. In Stoke, a patient has a panic attack in the ambulance.
Dr Fox is in the visitors' room for a change when his fiancee Sally has an operation to remove her wisdom teeth. Another patient has surgery to have a double hernia repaired.
One patient is flown home from holiday for an urgent operation to repair a detached retina, while another has a leaky vein that has caused an ulcer in his leg.
Not Everything Is Butterflies and Rainbows: The preceptorship midwives undergo bereavement training, while Aimee helps a family who have lost their baby.
